stories.title: The winners and losers from the UK’s trade deal with the Gulf
stories.summary: LONDON — For years, the U.K. has looked on enviously as the Gulf states used their vast, oil-derived wealth to build global economic influence. Now it’s moving to claim a slice of the pie. On Wednesday, Keir Starmer’s government achieved what three successive Tory administrations had failed to do: securing a trade deal with the six-nation Gulf Cooperation Council, with a combined wealth of around $2.3 trillion. It’s no small feat for Brexit Britain. While the GCC operates with a common external

stories.title: Many Nato countries not spending enough to support Ukraine, says Rutte – Europe live
stories.summary: Nato chief delivers speech in Sweden as he hints at further changes to US military commitments to Europe Just as Pavel was speaking in Prague, Russian foreign ministry spokesperson Maria Zakharova told reporters that Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy was pursuing escalation of the conflict between the two countries. It’s quite a claim given (checks notes) Russia’s continued and relentless invasion of Ukraine for years. “ Ukraine has demonstrated not only determination and heroism, but also

stories.title: Ukraine should be in NATO, Czech military chief says
stories.summary: The Czech Republic’s top general said Ukraine should join NATO in the future as the next “logical step.” “Ukraine is not just a security consumer, it’s also a security provider,” the chief of the Czech defense staff, Gen. Karel Řehka, told POLITICO’s Speakeasy on the sidelines of the GLOBSEC forum in Prague Thursday. “I’m not saying it’s easy. At the end of the day, it’s about political consensus, but I think that’s the direction

stories.title: Hungary to limit prime ministers to maximum eight-year terms
stories.summary: Péter Magyar’s draft amendment would prevent Viktor Orbán from returning to the role Europe live – latest updates Hungary’s new government, led by Péter Magyar, has put forward a constitutional amendment that would limit prime ministers to a maximum of eight years in office, in effect barring Viktor Orbán from returning to the role. The draft amendment was submitted on Wednesday, just over a week after the new government took office. It marked Magyar and his Tisza party’s first step in dismantli

stories.title: Latvia scrambles NATO jets in drone alert, the latest of several in Baltic
stories.summary: Latvia's military said NATO planes responded to at least one drone flying in its airspace. It's the latest in a string of such incidents in the Baltic, one of which eventually toppled Latvia's government.

stories.title: UK did not vet former Prince Andrew before making him trade envoy
stories.summary: LONDON — The British government did no formal vetting or due diligence before appointing the former Prince Andrew as a trade envoy, ministers have admitted.  The U.K. government on Thursday published a cache of documents relating to the appointment of the disgraced ex-royal as special representative for UK trade and investment in 2001. Lawmakers compelled the release of the documents in February as they seek to shed light on the thinking in government at the time of the decision. In an acco

stories.title: Europe must make big changes to compete with US, space chief warns
stories.summary: Europe has strong space assets but it is underfunded, too slow and too fragmented in comparison to the U.S., European Space Agency boss Josef Aschbacher warned on Thursday. “If we’re not making big changes, we will never catch up. We will always run behind, and this is, of course, a big risk,” he told POLITICO’s Speakeasy at the GLOBSEC forum in Prague. As American companies, most notably Elon Musk’s SpaceX, continue to dominate the market across technologies including rockets, satel

stories.title: EU complains about ‘surprise’ UK move to roll back Russia sanctions
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The EU’s economy chief criticized the U.K.’s decision to loosen planned sanctions on Russian-origin jet fuel and diesel without giving prior notice to fellow G7 allies. The U.K. issued a sanctions licence on Tuesday exempting jet fuel and diesel from a new import ban on oil products made from Russian crude but refined in India or other third countries, a decision which “came as a surprise,” according to Economy Commissioner Valdis Dombrovskis. During a press conference on Thursday, th

stories.title: Tennessee set to execute first person forced to represent himself at trial in more than a century
stories.summary: Tony Carruthers’s lawyers say no evidence tied him to 1994 crimes he was convicted of and is mentally incompetent Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Tennessee is scheduled on Thursday to execute a prison inmate whose lawyers claim there was no physical evidence tying him to the crimes he was convicted of and is mentally incompetent. Additionally, the inmate’s lawyers believe that the state may be using expired lethal injection drugs to carry out the sentence. Tony Carruthers, 57,

stories.title: Czechia: Uproar over Sudeten German gathering in Brno
stories.summary: The first gathering of Sudeten Germans in Czechia since World War II is set to go ahead this weekend despite a political backlash that has exposed tensions over one of Central Europe's most painful historical legacies.

stories.title: I, personally, demand to know what happened to those zebras
stories.summary: He suggested that Viktor Orbán deserved to be awarded the Order of Lenin for his pro-Russian policies, he cheered the elimination of the Druzhba oil pipeline, and feels that he emerged victorious from his war of words with Elon Musk—the Polish Foreign Minister is no stranger to blunt statements. He believes in a stronger Europe capable of defending itself, in the defeat of Russia—which launched a full-scale war against Ukraine four years ago—and in the restoration of the Polish-Hungarian relatio

stories.title: Last days of the cormorant: 10 EU countries want open season on fish-eating bird
stories.summary: The great cormorant has had a good run. Driven to the brink of extinction by hunters and pesticides in the 1970s, the fish-eating waterbird was rescued thanks to one of the EU’s earliest environmental laws. On paper, that regulation represents a triumph for conservationists: Over the past 50 years, the cormorant population has grown from roughly 50,000 birds to between 1.5 and 2 million across Europe. But 10 EU countries now consider the aquatic species a menace that needs to be contained.

stories.title: Video shows Lithuanians sheltering in underground parking during drone alert
stories.summary: Footage showed residents sheltering in an underground parking area after Lithuanian authorities issued a drone alert in Vilnius and urged people to move to safe locations amid reported activity near the Belarus border.

stories.title: 286 milliárd forint mínusz: nagyon úgy tűnik, hogy tényleg trükközött az Orbán-kormány az idei költségvetéssel
stories.summary: Magyar Péterék szerint „trükkök százait” bevetve hamisította meg az idei költségvetést az Orbán-kormány, a Fidesz szerint azonban szó sincs ilyenről. Megnéztük, megvan-e a költségvetésben az a három, összesen több százmilliárdos tétel, amelyről az átadás-átvételen szó volt.

stories.title: Uniós ügy lett Orbán peréből a 24.hu ellen, az EU bírósága két kérdésben tett igazságot
stories.summary: Orbán Viktor 2024-ben perelt be számos szerkesztőséget magánszemélyként, a 24.hu elleni ügynél kérdezte a Fővárosi Törvényszék az EU bíróságát.

stories.title: Fidesz is in bigger trouble than Viktor Orbán
stories.summary: The party needs three things if it is to renew itself, but it currently does not have much of any of them. We spoke with several leading Fidesz politicians about what they believe led to their election defeat and how the party should move forward.

stories.title: Lengyel Tamás szerint az Eszenyi-ügyben pontosan úgy viselkedett a főváros, mint a Fidesz
stories.summary: Lengyel szerint egy tiszás képviselő megígérte neki, hogy utánajár az Eszenyi-ügy vizsgálatának.

stories.title: Magyar Bécsben az azbesztügyről: A szennyező fizet elvét a végén Magyarország alkalmazni fogja
stories.summary: Lengyelország után Magyar Péter Bécsben tárgyalt. Christian Stockerrel beszéltek a különadókról, hétfőtől osztrák–magyar közös bizottság áll fel az azbesztszennyezés miatt. Magyar bejelentette, hogy ősszel Gödöllőn lesz közös kormányülés az osztrákokkal.

stories.title: Direct daily flights between Budapest and the United States resuming on Thursday
stories.summary: There are still round-trip tickets available for June, July, and September for just over 220,000 forints.

stories.title: Varga Judit váratlanul reagált a Telexnek, csak nem a kérdéseinkre
stories.summary: A volt igazságügyi minisztert többször kerestük azután, hogy Magyar Péter kormánya nyilvánosságra hozta a kegyelmi ügy dokumentációjának egyik felét.

stories.title: Személyesen is tudni akarom, hogy mi történt azokkal a zebrákkal
stories.summary: Lenin-érdemrendet javasolt Orbán Viktornak oroszbarát politikája miatt, szurkolt a Barátság kőolajvezeték kiiktatásának, úgy érzi, győztesen került ki a szópárbajból Elon Muskkal szemben – a lengyel külügyminiszter nem idegenkedik a karcos nyilatkozatoktól. Hisz az erősebb, önálló védelemre képes Európában, az Ukrajna ellen négy éve totális háborút indító Oroszország legyőzhetőségében és a lengyel–magyar kapcsolatok megjavításában. Radosław Sikorski adott interjút a Telexnek Magyar Péter varsói

stories.title: Egyre láthatóbb Orbánék költségvetési aknája, az Európai Bizottság 6,2 százalékos hiánnyal számol
stories.summary: Az uniós testület kiemelte: 2025 végén és 2026 elején, azaz az Orbán-kormány alatt több hiánynövelő intézkedést vezettek be. Már a beígért 5 százalék is problémásnak tűnt az uniós túlzottdeficit-eljárásban.

stories.title: Magyar in Vienna: Hungary will apply the polluter pays principle with regard to the asbestos contamination
stories.summary: In Vienna, Péter Magyar and Christian Stocker agreed to set up a joint Austrian-Hungarian committee to address the issue of asbestos contamination in some parts of Western Hungary, and Magyar announced that the two countries would hold a joint government meeting in Gödöllő this fall.

stories.title: Vitézy Dávid továbbfejlesztené a Budapest és Bécs közötti vasúti szolgáltatásokat
stories.summary: Vitézy az osztrák közlekedési miniszterrel tárgyalt, már júniusban újraindítják az együttműködést.

stories.title: Erre a piros vasalódeszkára cseréli a házelnöki széket Forsthoffer Ágnes
stories.summary: A döntést azzal magyarázta, hogy Tisza István házelnöki székének napi igénybevétele gyorsíthatja a kopást és sérüléseket okozhat.

stories.title: 16 nőjogi szervezet levélben kereste meg Magyar Pétert
stories.summary: Ebben azt kérik a miniszterelnöktől, állítson fel a kormány egy nemek közötti egyenlőségért felelős önálló intézményt.

stories.title: Eduline: Rekordszinten az SNI-s tanulók száma
stories.summary: Egyre több sajátos nevelési igényű diák tanul a magyar iskolákban, az intézmények és a szakemberek egyre nagyobb kihívásokkal szembesülnek az integrált oktatás biztosításában.

stories.title: Ismét drón repült be Lettországba, riasztották a NATO-vadászgépeket
stories.summary: Ez már a harmadik egymást követő nap, hogy Lettország drónveszélyre figyelmeztető riasztást ad ki.

stories.title: Jók a kilátások a források felszabadítására az Európai Bizottság biztosa szerint, de nem találgatna, mennyit sikerül felhasználni
stories.summary: Valdis Dombrovskis a Portfoliónak adott interjújában Orbánról, az euró bevezetéséről és a túlzott költségvetési hiány miatt 2024-ben indult eljárásról is beszélt.

stories.title: „Szerintünk meg nem” – vágott vissza Magyar arra, hogy a Fidesz szerint visszamenőleges jogalkotásba kezdtek
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök a külföldi útját lezáró rövid sajtótájékoztatón beszámolt arról is, hogy hova látogat el a következő hetekben.

stories.title: Egymásra vár a köztársasági elnök és a kormány a kegyelmi ügy iratainak kiadásával
stories.summary: Sulyok kiadná a náluk lévő aktát, ha jönne hivatalos megkeresés, a kormány szerint Magyar Péter már felszólította az elnököt rá.

stories.title: Lássuk, hogy kerülnek vissza titokban a forgalomba a totálkáros autók
stories.summary: Egy biztosítási kárügyintézéssel foglalkozó cég szakértője szerint az állam közönye miatt virágzik a roncspiac Magyarországon, a törött autókat sokszor filléres módszerekkel kalapálják össze, majd újra eladják őket.

stories.title: Nick Hornby Budapestre látogat fiaival, hogy lássa kedvenc csapatát a Bajnokok Ligája döntőjében játszani
stories.summary: A Fociláz és a Pop, csajok, satöbbi szerzője az Arsenal miatt érkezik a fővárosba.

stories.title: Lithuania's leaders take shelter during drone air alert
stories.summary: Flights were suspended and people were urged to take shelter after a suspected drone was detected near the Belarus border.

stories.title: Hungary's new Prime Minister Magyar seeks to fix Poland ties
stories.summary: Peter Magyar hopes to mend relations with Poland during his first state visit to Warsaw. What lessons could he learn from Poland's Prime Minister Donald Tusk?

stories.title: Arsenal win the Premier League ending 22-year wait after Manchester City draw with Bournemouth
stories.summary: The Gunners have finally secured a Premier League title ending a 22-year wait for the coveted title after Manchester City drew with Bournemouth. The north Londoners can now set their sights on winning their first Champions League final in Budapest, where they face holders Paris Saint-Germain.

stories.title: NATO planes down suspected Ukrainian drone over Estonia
stories.summary: A NATO military jet has shot down a suspected Ukrainian drone in Estonian airspace. As Ukraine targets Russia's Baltic oil facilities more often and more intensely, more such drones are straying into allies' territory.

stories.title: Sweden orders 4 frigates from French company Naval Group
stories.summary: Sweden plans to acquire four new frigates starting in 2030 in a deal worth billions. They will be the largest surface warships in Sweden's navy. The newest NATO member is rushing to build up its military capabilities.

stories.title: Could Romania and Moldova reunite?
stories.summary: Moldovan leader Maia Sandu and her Romanian counterpart Nicusor Dan want to reunite their countries. Is that a realistic scenario?

stories.title: Young Czechs drink less beer but risk other addictions
stories.summary: The Czech Republic, the beer world champion, has seen a record decline in consumption. Younger generations are drinking less. Breweries are worried, but health experts say it's good news.

stories.title: Democrats release 2024 presidential campaign autopsy; Republicans could abandon $1bn proposal for Trump’s ballroom – live
stories.summary: DNC chair Ken Martin apologizes for delay in releasing report; Republican senators have queried timing and lack of detail on security bill additions Sign up for the Breaking News US email Senate Democratic leader Chuck Schumer, House Democratic leader Hakeem Jeffries and other Democrats spoke held a news conference, ahead of the vote-a-rama Thursday morning. “The Republican agenda is one big broken promise,” said Schumer, criticizing the Republican budget bill. “We still haven’t seen the bill, b

stories.title: Russia pledges to support Cuba as Trump ramps up pressure on Havana
stories.summary: Russia will provide Cuba with “active support” as the U.S. increases pressure on Havana, Kremlin foreign ministry spokeswoman Maria Zakharova said Thursday. Washington’s move to cut off oil shipments from Venezuela and its threats to punish companies doing business with Cuba has caused a dire energy crisis on the island. On Wednesday, tensions between the two countries escalated further after the U.S. Justice Department announced it was bringing charges against former Cuban Pre

stories.title: One Nation reveals gas policy – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Pauline Hanson announces Norway-inspired gas policy as she decries 25% export tax as ‘economic vandalism’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Albanese leaves door open to extending fuel excise cut The prime minister, Anthony Albanese , has not ruled out extending the temporary cut to the fuel excise as the Middle East conflict continues to disrupt the global oil market. We’ll do an assessment closer to the day. It is very costly but it has made a

stories.title: UK summons Israel’s chargé d’affaires over video of minister taunting activists
stories.summary: Foreign Office denounces treatment of Gaza flotilla activists as outcry over Itamar Ben-Gvir’s video escalates The UK has summoned Israel’s chargé d’affaires as international outrage escalates over a video posted by the national security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, in which he is seen taunting activists detained after a Gaza-bound flotilla was intercepted . The global outcry continued as Israel began releasing hundreds of the activists who attempted to breach Israel’s naval blockade of Gaza. Auth

stories.title: Bryant says there was ‘absolutely nothing’ in Andrew documents to suggest ministers knew of his friendship with Epstein – UK politics live
stories.summary: Chris Bryant gives statement to parliament following the release of documents today The Home Office has also published asylum figures this morning. These show that the number of asylum seekers being housed temporarily in hotels stood at 20,885 at the end of March 2026, down 35% year-on-year from 32,326. The Press Association says: It is the lowest figure since data was first reported in 2022, Home Office figures show. The total had climbed as high as 56,018 at the end of September 2023. Brits ar

stories.title: Turkey: DW's Alican Uludag released, but trial continues
stories.summary: A Turkish court has allowed DW reporter Alican Uludag to leave detention as his contentious trial opens. He is accused of having "insulted" President Recep Tayyip Erdogan, which in Turkey can carry a jail sentence.

stories.title: Ukraine strikes Russian oil refinery in long-range drone attack, Kyiv says
stories.summary: Ukraine has been ramping up its retaliatory long-range strikes on Russia in recent weeks, particularly targeting oil infrastructure.

stories.title: Israel says all Gaza flotilla activists have been deported amid global outrage over their treatment
stories.summary: Multiple European countries are calling for EU sanctions against Israeli national security minister Itamar Ben Gvir.

stories.title: UK strikes £3.7bn trade deal with six Gulf states
stories.summary: Keir Starmer describes the agreement, worth double original estimates, as a ‘huge win’ for British businesses Keir Starmer has struck a trade deal with six Gulf states in what he described as a huge win for British business, ending four years of talks led by four different prime ministers. The deal will offer £3.7bn worth of opportunities for exporters – double the original estimates – particularly in the food and luxury car sectors but also defence, aerospace, hospitality and other services, th

stories.title: US strengthens Greenland presence with new consulate
stories.summary: The U.S. will open a new consulate in Nuuk on Thursday, deepening its presence on the island as the White House sharpens its focus on the Arctic. The inauguration of the new, 3,000-square-meter complex in Nuuk’s city center has prompted protests from native Greenlanders, with the island’s premier, Jens-Frederik Nielsen, telling local outlet Sermitsiaq that he will not attend the event. Trump reopened the American consulate in 2020 during his first term, but it initially had to be housed in a Dan

stories.title: Pedro Sánchez is loved everywhere – but not so much in Spain. Here’s why | María Ramírez
stories.summary: The PM’s socialist party was crushed in its former Andalusian stronghold. With a general election in 2027, time to turn things around is running out Lately, I often meet people outside Spain who praise the prime minister, Pedro Sánchez. In Britain, Italy or the US, friends, acquaintances or random people who learn I am Spanish offer admiring words about his positions on Gaza and Iran. It’s understandable. Sánchez spoke out against Benjamin Netanyahu and Donald Trump earlier and more forcefully t

The proposal for the commonwealth to co-own

stories.title: Colombia’s climate crossroads: Trumpism casts shadow over presidential battle
stories.summary: Colombia is a global leader in climate activism. Could US influence drag country to a future of mining and fracking? Several hours after dark in a quiet Caribbean neighbourhood, a cluster of environmental activists gather on plastic chairs between a mango tree and a courtyard wall emblazoned with the words “Colombia, respira!” (Breathe, Colombia). So many people have turned up that some have to stand. That is because tonight’s speaker is Susana Muhamad , one of the most admired socio-environment

stories.title: Record number of dams dismantled in Europe in effort to help wildlife thrive
stories.summary: Weirs, culverts and sluices among 602 barriers demolished in year in attempt to restore 15,500 miles of rivers by 2030 A few miles downstream from a lava field in western Iceland, the gargle of free-flowing water is unbroken for the first time in decades after hydraulic peckers chipped away at a dilapidated dam that once powered a farm. The structure on the River Melsá had continued to block fish migration long after falling into disrepair. “It wasn’t providing any electricity; the old power hou

stories.title: Parody Cockroach Janta political party’s rise reflects youth anger in India
stories.summary: Satirical project is viral sensation and outlet for protest on social media as it taps into young people’s frustration It began as a satirical online project after a high court judge compared unemployed young people to cockroaches. Now millions of young Indians are flocking to it as an outlet for their frustration. A parody political party with the insect as its symbol has exploded across India’s social media by turning absurdist humour into protest. Memes and short videos mocking corruption, jo

stories.title: Queen’s ‘keenness’ for Andrew to be trade envoy was a grave mistake
stories.summary: The monarch may have thought the role would keep her ‘favourite’ second son out of trouble. How wrong she was No evidence of formal security vetting when Andrew became UK trade envoy, minister says That Queen Elizabeth II was “very keen” for Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor to take on a “prominent role in the promotion of national interests” as a trade envoy in 2001 demonstrates the fierce support the late monarch always gave her second son. Knowing he was “the spare”, and undoubtedly acutely aware of

stories.title: MPs urged to ignore fearmongering and pass Labor’s ‘long overdue’ negative gearing and CGT changes
stories.summary: Exclusive: Any attempt to use the budget measures as an excuse to raise rent is opportunistic profiteering, housing advocates say Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s peak community and housing groups have urged federal parliament to quickly pass Labor’s changes to negative gearing and capital gains tax, saying the reforms would improve fairness for renters and young homebuyers. The government may introduce its negative gearing and CGT changes into parliament

stories.title: WiseTech begins redundancies – but omits ‘AI’ from emails to Chinese employees, workers say
stories.summary: ASX-listed company announced in February it would lay off almost 30% of its 7,000-strong workforce across 40 countries Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast WiseTech has begun informing staff that they will lose their jobs as part of redundancies the company has said is due to artificial intelligence advancements – although an email to staff in China omitted the word “AI” after a court case against another company in the country. Staff at WiseTech have been waiting almost

stories.title: New Orleans prosecutors file formal battery charges against Shia LaBeouf
stories.summary: Prosecutors opt not to pursue hate-crime charges over February incident despite anti-gay slurs captured on video New Orleans state prosecutors on Thursday filed formal misdemeanor battery charges against Shia LaBeouf , four months after police officers there arrested him on allegations that he struck three men at a bar. That move from the office of local district attorney Jason Williams means prosecutors opted to not pursue hate-crime charges against LaBeouf, the star of the Transformers film fr

stories.title: Macron under pressure over reparatory justice for France’s role in slave trade
stories.summary: Demands grow for launch of formal discussion process on how country should address legacies of enslavement Emmanuel Macron is under pressure to open discussions on reparatory justice for France’s role in hundreds of years of enslavement of African people as he makes a key speech on the legacy of slavery. On Thursday the French president will celebrate the 25th anniversary of France becoming the first country in the world to recognise the slave trade and slavery as crimes against humanity in a 20

stories.title: Senate Republicans expected to ditch $1bn funding plan for Trump’s ballroom
stories.summary: Top Republicans fear diverting taxpayer dollars toward the White House ballroom will alienate voters before midterms Senate Republican leaders are expected to ditch a $1bn proposal for security measures tied to Donald Trump’s White House ballroom following a backlash from members of their own party. Under pressure from Trump, top Republican lawmakers tried to latch the proposal on to a roughly $70bn bill to restore funding to US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and the border patrol. Co

stories.title: Bolivia rocked by protests as US warns of ‘coup d’état’
stories.summary: Clashes between demonstrators and police in La Paz have entered second week, shaking centre-right president Protests blocking roads across Bolivia and turning the centre of the capital, La Paz, into a battleground between demonstrators and police have entered a second week. It is the most turbulent moment of the centre-right president Rodrigo Paz Pereira’s mere six months in office since he ended nearly two decades of rule by the leftwing Movimiento al Socialismo (Mas). Continue reading...

stories.title: Philippines orders arrest of senator over ICC 'war on drugs' charges
stories.summary: Senator Ronald "Bato" dela Rosa was Rodrigo Duterte's top enforcer during the deadly "war on drugs" which saw thousands killed.

stories.title: Germany news: Govt reportedly mulls change to retirement age
stories.summary: A media report that the German government wants to raise the age of retirement has come under hefty criticism. Meanwhile, Chancellor Friedrich Merz wants to grant Ukraine special status. DW has more.

stories.title: Is the US deepening its military involvement in Nigeria?
stories.summary: Airstrikes in Nigeria led by the US have weakened the so-called Islamic State (IS) terror group. The joint operation shows an expanding US role, but analysts warn of limited long‑term impact and civilian risks.

stories.title: US raises pressure on Cuba as it indicts former President Raul Castro on murder charges
stories.summary: Trump called the indictment of Castro a “very big moment” but dismissed plans of the US taking military action on the island nation as they had with Venezuela in recent months.

stories.summary: After four years of Russia's full-scale invasion, Ukraine has become far more successful at fighting off air raids.

stories.summary: The walkout, which was due to start on Thursday, has been suspended while union members vote on a tentative deal.

stories.summary: Video released by Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir showed detained Gaza flotilla activists handcuffed on the ground at Ashdod port.

stories.title: Trump envoy says US should restore its 'footprint' in Greenland
stories.summary: The US is reported to have had 17 military facilities and more than 10,000 troops on Greenland at the height of the Cold War.

stories.title: Tehran politicians are holding back military from striking US fleet, Iranian MP tells Euronews
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview, a senior member of Iran's parliamentary National Security Commission tells Euronews that political authorities have actively restrained the military from attacking US naval assets — but warns Tehran will sink a US warship if that check is gone.

stories.title: Israel destroyed a four storey house in overnight airstrike in central Gaza camp
stories.summary: Israel destroyed a four storey house in Gaza’s Maghazi refugee camp after residents received a warning call ordering evacuation before the strike.

stories.title: Gaza flotilla activists set for deportation from Israel as backlash over treatment grows
stories.summary: Activists have begun to leave Israel after international outrage over their treatment in detention.

stories.title: Hamas to blame for lack of progress on phase two of ceasefire, Board of Peace report says
stories.summary: Despite an October ceasefire, Gaza remains gripped by daily violence as Israeli strikes continue, with both the military and Hamas accusing one another of violating the truce.

stories.title: EU cuts growth forecasts as Middle East war drags on
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission on Thursday downgraded economic growth estimates to 1.1 percent, as surging energy prices fueled by the Middle East war cause a slowdown. In its latest economic forecasts, the EU executive said the conflict will result in higher inflation and public debt across the bloc, as it reduced its expected growth for the EU’s economy from 1.4 to 1.1 percent this year. But things could get worse yet. The economic growth estimates might be halved if the conflict cont

stories.title: ‘Indefensible’: alleged child abuse survivor takes on Albanese government over $2.5bn Nauru deal
stories.summary: Lawyers of Hazara man who was allegedly sexually abused by carer launch bid to prevent imminent deportation to Pacific island Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Albanese government’s $2.5bn deal with Nauru, under which hundreds of non-citizens will be sent to the tiny Pacific island, will face another legal challenge prompted by an alleged child abuse survivor. Legal representatives for Abdul*, a Hazara man who

stories.title: US is ‘simply choosing not to stop’ Ebola outbreak after massive public health cuts, experts say
stories.summary: Hundreds of cases reported in the DRC after USAID has been dismantled and key scientific research canceled A previously undetected outbreak of Ebola is coursing through parts of central Africa, and the US appears to be doing little to help stop it, after massive cuts to global and domestic public health efforts. There is no cure and no vaccine for the rare Bundibugyo variant of Ebola, which has caused two outbreaks in recent decades. Health leaders and scientists are now racing to understand whe

stories.title: Mick Jagger to play Josh O’Connor’s father in new film from Alice Rohrwacher
stories.summary: The Rolling Stone will play a lighthouse keeper in Three Incestuous Sisters, joining a cast including Dakota Johnson, Jessie Buckley and Saoirse Ronan Rolling Stones singer Mick Jagger is playing a lighthouse keeper in the new film from Happy as Lazzaro director Alice Rohrwacher , which is currently filming on the Italian island of Stromboli. According to reports in the Italian media , Jagger was photographed on arrival in Stromboli after flying in by helicopter to take a role in Three Incestuou

stories.title: Girl, two, dies after being left in car as extreme heat sweeps Spain
stories.summary: Authorities in Galicia declare two days of mourning after toddler dies during exceptionally high May temperatures Europe live – latest updates A two-year-old girl has died of heatstroke in north-west Spain after being accidentally left in her father’s car during an unseasonably hot spell that could push temperatures in some areas to 38C (100F). The child, who has not been named, went into cardiac arrest on Wednesday afternoon after spending several hours inside the vehicle in the Galician town o

stories.title: EasyJet summer holiday bookings down on last year amid Iran war uncertainty
stories.summary: Airline, which took £25m hit on jet fuel in March, says passengers are waiting later to book trips Business live – latest updates The airline easyJet has said its summer holiday bookings are lagging behind last year’s, as the Middle East conflict weighs on consumer confidence and passengers appear to be waiting later to book trips. The budget carrier said it had to spend an unexpected extra £25m on jet fuel in March after the start of the US-Israel war on Iran. Continue reading...

stories.title: Record 274 climbers summit Everest from Nepalese side in single day
stories.summary: Climbers take advantage of clear weather after threat of ice fall on normal route delayed start of spring season A record 274 climbers have reached the summit of Mount Everest from the Nepalese side in a single day after a spring season that started late because of the threat of ice fall on the normal tourist route. The climbers took advantage of the clear weather on Wednesday, said Rishi Ram Bhandari, of the Expedition Operators Association Nepal. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘It’s broken English’: MP’s attempt to speak Jamaican in parliament sparks language row
stories.summary: Parliamentary rule that only English is allowed has reignited debate about language, legitimacy and postcolonial identity When the Jamaican MP Nekeisha Burchell stood up to give her maiden speech, she was keenly aware of how much her country’s parliament mirrored the Westminster version thousands of miles away in London. As in the UK, the session on 12 May had started with the arrival of the ceremonial mace – a 1.7-metre ornamented silver staff representing the British monarch’s authority over p

stories.title: Air France and Airbus guilty of corporate manslaughter for 2009 plane crash
stories.summary: Firms given maximum fine of €225,000 each and are expected to appeal after lower court had cleared them A Paris appeals court has found Airbus and Air France guilty of corporate manslaughter over the 2009 Rio-Paris plane crash that killed 228 passengers and crew. The verdict is the latest milestone in a legal marathon involving two of France’s most emblematic companies and families of the mainly French, Brazilian and German victims of France’s worst air disaster. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘It’s put the joy levels up’: the flood-prone London school with a climate-adapted playground
stories.summary: When pupils could no longer play outside, St John’s school in Barnet decided to act, enlisting Trees for Cities to help rethink its outside space The play area at St John’s Church of England primary in Barnet, north London, used to flood so severely it was often unusable. “It would get so bad that the children couldn’t be dismissed from the playground,” says Macci Dobie, the school’s headteacher. “We had to dismiss them from different parts of the school or, literally, parents were stepping into

stories.title: NHS trust sacks staff who illegally accessed records of Nottingham attack victims
stories.summary: Nottingham university hospitals trust says 11 members of staff dismissed and 14 others given written warnings An NHS trust has sacked 11 staff members who illegally accessed the medical records of the victims of the Nottingham stabbing attacks. Valdo Calocane killed two 19-year-old students, Barnaby Webber and Grace O’Malley-Kumar, and Ian Coates, a 65-year-old caretaker, and attempted to kill three other people in the city in June 2023. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump’s EPA to roll back refrigerant rule for grocery stores in push it claims will lower prices
stories.summary: EPA is loosening Biden-era rule that requires US businesses to reduce greenhouse gases used in cooling equipment The Trump administration is set to loosen a federal rule that requires grocery stores and air-conditioning companies to reduce greenhouse gases used in cooling equipment, in what officials say is a push to lower grocery costs. The head of the Environmental Protection Agency, Lee Zeldin, said the Biden-era rule imposes costly restrictions that limit the type of refrigerants US business

stories.title: One Nation’s rapid national expansion in disarray as ‘significant risks’ force dissolution of new branches
stories.summary: Exclusive: Documents seen by Guardian Australia also show new branches and members will be subject to strict gag orders Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast One Nation’s rapid expansion of local branches across the country is in disarray, with the party being forced to dissolve and re-establish its new network less than eight months after the ambitious roll out began. Documents seen by Guardian Australia show the party’s new general manager, Kelvin Morton, issued a direct

stories.title: No evidence of formal security vetting when Andrew became UK trade envoy, minister says
stories.summary: Documents released by government also show late queen was ‘very keen’ for her son to have prominent role UK politics live – latest updates Formal security vetting and due diligence appear not to have been carried out before the appointment of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor as a trade envoy, the government has said, as it emerged that the late queen was “very keen” for her son to take a prominent role in promoting Britain’s interests. The first batch of documents relating to the appointment of the th

stories.title: Canada faces calls for investigation into death of woman after plasma donation
stories.summary: International student Rodiyat Alabede, 22, died due to a ‘perfect storm’ of lax safety protocols, advocates say Patient advocates in Canada have called for a new investigation into the death of a young woman who was donating blood plasma, describing a “perfect storm” of lax safety protocols and poorly trained staff and warning of “systemic issues” at plasma donation sites across the country. Rodiyat Alabede, an international student at the University of Winnipeg, died of cardiac arrest shortly a

stories.title: Papua New Guinea warns against fishing in New Ireland after mystery deaths of marine life
stories.summary: Initial testing found evidence of metals in water samples, months after province’s residents began reporting unusual numbers of dead fish washing ashore Papua New Guinea’s government has warned communities not to fish from parts of the New Ireland coastline as preliminary tests show evidence of metals in some water samples, after months of residents reporting dead marine life in the area. On 7 May the fisheries minister, Jelta Wong, said initial testing conducted by an independent company detect

stories.title: India news: Cockroaches overtake Modi's BJP on Instagram
stories.summary: Millions of young Indians are following the Cockroach Janata Party Instagram account, symbolic of growing frustration towards the current political system. Meanwhile, Delhi sizzles at 46 degrees Celcius. DW has more

stories.title: Iran war updates: Pakistan's army chief to visit Tehran
stories.summary: Pakistani Field Marshal Asim Munir is set to visit Iran as a peace mediator, Iranian media say. Tehran says it is reviewing Washington's latest proposals to end the war. DW has more.

stories.title: Extreme climate scenario fades, but warming continues
stories.summary: The rapid rollout of renewable energy is helping to shift emissions trends, but expected temperature rises remain high as the UN moves to tighten countries' commitments.

stories.title: Taiwan: Trump prods China with offer of direct Taipei talks
stories.summary: Donald Trump has said he will talk with Taiwan's president about purchasing US arms. The move would break over four decades of protocol and be an affront to China.

stories.title: Thailand revives bill to clear the air of toxic smog
stories.summary: Seasonal smog is a major public health problem in Thailand. Long-delayed legislation aims to stop air pollution before it starts, but enforcement could present a problem.

stories.title: Germany's Merz pitches 'associate' EU membership for Ukraine
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has proposed giving Ukraine interim EU membership without voting rights. He stressed that he still wanted Ukraine to eventually become a "full member."

stories.title: French court finds Airbus, Air France guilty in 2009 crash
stories.summary: A Paris appeals court has found Airbus and Air ‌France ⁠guilty ⁠of corporate manslaughter over a 2009 air crash. The disaster in the Atlantic Ocean claimed the lives of all on board Air France Flight 447.

stories.title: Ebola patient in Berlin: Why Germany's helping
stories.summary: An Ebola‑infected American doctor is being treated under the highest safety standards in a specialized isolation unit in the German capital, Berlin. The facilities are considered to be among the best equipped worldwide.

stories.title: Will SpaceX's IPO fund life on Mars — and a trillionaire?
stories.summary: SpaceX is launching history's biggest public listing, propelling Elon Musk's wealth into the stratosphere. DW explores whether the firm's out-of-this-world ambitions are truly cosmic or another black hole for investors.

stories.title: New Chinese surveillance leaves foreigners nowhere to hide
stories.summary: A Chinese cybersecurity expert has revealed to DW details of China's new high-tech policing. From ski resort facial recognition to seats on a train, the system can track anyone and compile a "holistic profile."

stories.title: Kevin Warsh: Trump's man at the Fed or independent voice?
stories.summary: Never before has a chair of the US Federal Reserve been so controversial. Who is Kevin Warsh? Can he preserve the Fed's independence, or will he become Donald Trump's puppet?

stories.title: Ebola: Experts warn of scale and spread of deadly virus
stories.summary: Health experts have warned the world is seeing just the "top of the iceberg" as the deadly Ebola outbreak spreads. Meanwhile, India and the African Union have postponed a scheduled summit due to the health emergency.

stories.title: Germany reaches out to Gulf states about Iran war
stories.summary: Germany no longer believes it can play a mediating role in the conflict between the US, Israel and Iran. Instead, the German government is working to strengthen its ties with the Gulf states.

stories.title: Montenegro at 20: EU ambitions and Serbian ties
stories.summary: Twenty years after seceding from the state union with Serbia, Montenegro is seen as a Western Balkan frontrunner for EU membership but remains marked by corruption, unresolved divisions and Serbia's political influence.

stories.title: EU pays Egypt billions to manage migration — Cairo says it is not enough
stories.summary: Egypt’s growing refugee and migrant population, seeking safety from wars in Sudan, Syria and the wider region, has put further pressure on aid agencies and public services as Cairo urges Europe to share more of the cost.

stories.title: Australian court fines Musk's X close to €400,000 for not disclosing data to online safety watchdog
stories.summary: The initial violation notice was sent by Australian authorities to Twitter Inc shortly before billionaire Elon Musk acquired the company and renamed it to X. The social media platform’s attorneys argued that the fine was not justified as the violation came during a period of change and transition.

stories.title: Trump sets tone on Taiwan after Xi summit, signals talks
stories.summary: Trump last week issued a warning to Taiwan against formally declaring independence from China.

stories.title: Lebanon rescuers search rubble after attacks in Tyre province
stories.summary: Rescuers search rubble in southern Lebanon after Israeli strikes kill at least 19, including women and children, despite an extended ceasefire.

stories.title: Bangladesh: Albino buffalo nicknamed “Donald Trump” becomes unlikely star attraction
stories.summary: An albino buffalo nicknamed “Donald Trump” draws huge crowds near Dhaka ahead of Eid al-Adha, as visitors flock to see its rare blond coat.

stories.title: Sacred hall housing 'eternal flame' in Japan destroyed by fire
stories.summary: The temple has moved the flame to a different location and authorities said the fire may have been caused by it, according to Japan Times.

stories.title: White House denies blocking Ebola evacuation
stories.summary: The White House has denied a report claiming that the United States blocked the return of Ebola-infected missionary doctor Peter Stafford.

stories.title: Jeanette revives cult film anthem "Por qué te vas" in Cannes event
stories.summary: Jeanette, the voice behind the iconic song Porque te vas from 1976’s Cría Cuervos, led celebrations in Cannes as France marked the film’s 50th anniversary with a restored screening and live performance at the Cannes Film Festival.

stories.title: Iran secretly executes four as it hits global death sentence record
stories.summary: Four more men, including two Kurdish political activists, were put to death in secret this week without informing their families, as Amnesty International recorded 2,159 executions in Iran last year — the highest of any country.

stories.title: Watch: 'Everybody back up!' Moment car explodes in NYC
stories.summary: Viral footage circulating online shows flames and thick black smoke enveloping the street in New York.

stories.title: Who is Raúl Castro: the man who inherited the revolution and still shapes Cuba under pressure
stories.summary: Fidel Castro’s brother and a central pillar of Cuban power for decades, the former leader is back in the spotlight after a US indictment, amid mounting pressure on the regime.

stories.title: Nvidia's latest record earnings fail to impress investors
stories.summary: The chip giant reported more stellar results but its shares fell after-hours as investors wonder if it can keep up its pace of growth amid greater competition.

stories.title: China says US should stop 'threats' against Cuba after ex-leader charged
stories.summary: It comes after the US charged Cuba's ex-leader with murder, the latest move putting pressure on the regime.

stories.title: Gaza students sit exams in tents as nearly all schools lie in ruins
stories.summary: With 658,000 children locked out of classrooms for two consecutive years, Gaza's official secondary exams begin next month. Teachers and students told Euronews what it means to prepare without textbooks, chairs — or walls.

stories.title: Gonorrhoea and syphilis hit record levels in Europe
stories.summary: STIs have surged thanks to record cases and gaps in testing and prevention, a health agency reports.

stories.title: Pashinyan grabs megaphone as exchange turns heated during election campaign
stories.summary: Armenian's election campaign turns fiery as Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan grabbed a megaphone and confronted critics accusing him of giving up Karabakh, during an election campaign event in Yerevan.

stories.title: Ukrainian drone pilots compete in high-speed contests
stories.summary: Ukraine is turning FPV drone training into competitive tournaments to boost pilots’ speed, precision and tactics in its war against Russia.

stories.title: Trump says he will speak to Taiwan's president in break from protocol
stories.summary: It comes as the US weighs whether to proceed with a $14bn arms sales to Taiwan, which China claims as its own.

stories.title: Dozens of vehicles burnt as Mali jihadists enforce blockade ahead of Eid holiday
stories.summary: The blockade has led to shortages of fuel and food ahead of next week's Muslim holiday of Eid.

stories.title: Intensifying heat wave grips northern India as temperatures near 48°C
stories.summary: India’s northern plains are enduring another intense heat wave, with authorities warning of prolonged extreme temperatures close to 45 degrees Celsius in New Delhi on Thursday. Cooling shelters and health alerts have been introduced across New Delhi and nearby states.

stories.title: DR Congo cancels World Cup training camp over Ebola outbreak
stories.summary: The outbreak, caused by a rare Ebola species, is thought to have caused 139 deaths so far.

stories.title: Who is Ahmad Vahidi, Interpol-wanted IRGC general and key Iranian war strategist?
stories.summary: Ahmad Vahidi, deputy IRGC commander and the man Argentina formally accused of planning the 1994 Buenos Aires bombing that killed 85 people, has emerged as the key figure in organising Tehran's military response and partaking in back-channel negotiations with regional mediators.

stories.title: Iran steps up claim to control Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Iran publishes a map claiming "armed forces oversight" across more than 22,000 sq km of the waterway.

stories.title: What is Ebola and why is stopping the latest outbreak so difficult?
stories.summary: An outbreak of Ebola in DR Congo involves a rare strain and is in an area affected by conflict.

stories.title: 'Historic' UK trade deal with Gulf states set to add billions to British economy
stories.summary: The deal is the first of its kind between the GCC and a G7 country, and will scrap tariffs on billions of euros worth of British exports.

stories.title: Air France and Airbus found guilty of manslaughter over 2009 plane crash
stories.summary: The companies were found guilty by a French court over an air disaster which killed 228 people.

stories.summary: Police said the vehicle was abandoned by the driver after it took on water near the shoreline of Grapevine Lake in north Texas.

stories.title: Watch: Why is the US going after Cuba's Raúl Castro 30 years on?
stories.summary: BBC correspondent Will Grant reports from Havana, hours after the US charged former Cuban leader with conspiracy to kill US nationals and other crimes.

stories.title: US and Israel ‘hoped to install Ahmadinejad as Iran’s leader’
stories.summary: Airstrike at the start of the war was aimed at freeing populist ex-president from house arrest, US newspaper claims Middle East crisis – live updates Fresh questions have been raised over the US and Israeli effort to depose the Iranian regime after it was claimed that Israel wanted to put the populist Mahmoud Ahmadinejad in power. Ahmadinejad’s turbulent presidency, from 2005 to 2013, was marked by incendiary attacks on Israel but he recast himself as a critic of the regime and champion of the p

stories.title: Are Xi and Putin still ‘best friends’? – The Latest
stories.summary: Xi Jinping welcomed Vladimir Putin to Beijing with pomp and pageantry, just days after hosting Donald Trump. But as Russia’s war in Ukraine makes Moscow increasingly dependent on China, and western leaders thaw relations with Beijing, what does the power imbalance mean for Xi and Putin’s relationship? Lucy Hough speaks to the Guardian’s deputy head of international news, Devika Bhat Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump may see himself in Ahmadinejad but having him lead Iran was a perplexing idea
stories.summary: Former Iranian president has a populist, headline-grabbing communication style but is an avowed anti-Zionist. How could Israel see him as a man to do business with? US and Israel hoped to install Ahmadinejad as Iran’s leader For all their outward differences, there always seemed to be things that linked Mahmoud Ahmadinejad and Donald Trump. A visit to the then Iranian president’s rather humble Tehran neighbourhood nearly 20 years ago highlighted cost of living problems that prefigured those faci

stories.title: Russian jet filmed flying within metres of RAF spy plane – video
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence has released footage that it says shows Russian jets flying dangerously close to an RAF spy plane last month. A Russian Su-35 jet flew sufficiently close to the Rivet Joint aircraft to trigger its emergency systems. In another incident, a Su-27 conducted six passes in front of the same aircraft, flying close to its nose and risking a collision Russian jet causes ‘dangerous’ near collision after flying close to RAF spy plane Continue reading...

stories.title: US indicts former Cuban president Raúl Castro as it seeks to oust regime
stories.summary: Charges filed in Miami against 94-year-old for allegedly shooting down exiles’ planes in 1996 The United States issued a federal criminal indictment against Raúl Castro , Cuba ’s former president, and five others on Wednesday in a significant escalation of the Trump administration’s campaign to oust the country’s six-decades-old communist regime. The 94-year-old political figurehead was charged in Miami, Florida, with conspiracy to kill US nationals, four counts of murder and two counts of destr

stories.title: Israeli security minister stirs diplomatic outrage with flotilla activist abuse video
stories.summary: Far-right figure Itamar Ben-Gvir shares footage of himself taunting bound international detainees Israel’s far-right national security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, has sparked a diplomatic crisis by publishing footage of Israeli security forces abusing international activists who were detained as they tried to sail to Gaza with aid. Three activists were taken to hospital as result of Israeli violence, lawyers representing the group said. They were subsequently discharged. Dozens of others have sus

stories.title: Murder or accident? Mystery of Mango tycoon's hiking death after son's arrest
stories.summary: Isak Andic's son Jonathan denies involvement in the fatal fall of his father, who founded one of Europe's biggest clothing empires.

stories.title: Whale to be removed from Danish island after failed German rescue
stories.summary: Danish officials will conduct a post mortem examination on the whale, which was released into the sea from a barge more than two weeks ago.

stories.title: Qatar’s tourism sector shows resilience and is ready to welcome visitors again
stories.summary: In an interview with Euronews, Visit Qatar CEO Abdulaziz Ali Al-Mawlawi revealed that the Gulf state repatriated 12,000 international visitors during the height of the recent regional tensions.

stories.title: Rosenberg: Putin enjoys Xi's Chinese welcome but heads home without pipeline deal
stories.summary: Russia and China showed they were shoulder-to-shoulder on the world stage, but it became clear there are limits, says the BBC's Russia Editor.

stories.title: Rare aardvark born at UK’s Chester Zoo
stories.summary: Baby aardvark Womble is being hand-fed by keepers at Chester Zoo after a rare birth, only the second in the zoo’s 94-year history in the UK.

stories.title: Trump calls Castro indictment 'very big moment' but says no need for escalation
stories.summary: An unsealed indictment accused Castro and others of murder, conspiracy to kill Americans and destruction of aircraft.

stories.title: Final two bodies of missing Italians recovered from inside Maldives cave
stories.summary: The two were among five Italian nationals who drowned in last week's scuba diving accident in the Maldives.

stories.title: No country has ever developed without urbanising, World Bank economist tells Euronews at Baku forum
stories.summary: Urbanisation drives economic growth and job creation, a World Bank senior expert told Euronews at the World Urban Forum in Baku, but warned that Africa faces a financing gap that no single institution can fill alone.

stories.title: Activists use giant Tedros and Bill Gates balloons at World Health Organization protest
stories.summary: Activists protest the WHO pandemic treaty in Geneva with giant Tedros and Gates balloons, accusing the deal of threatening national sovereignty.

stories.title: New video captures engine ripping off cargo plane in deadly Kentucky crash
stories.summary: As part of the ongoing investigation into the crash that killed 14 people, officials released footage showing the engine detaching during takeoff.

stories.title: Austrian ex-intelligence officer found guilty of Russia spying charges
stories.summary: The spy scandal has revived fears that Austria remains a hotbed of Russian espionage activity.

Már nemcsak az a kérdés, mennyi áramot használnak, hanem az is, hogy melyik napszakban. stories.title: Csak a pénzen múlott, hogy Velence mintájára most nem hajókkal van teli az Oktogon
stories.summary: Naponta ezrek sietnek át rajta, pedig az Oktogon jóval több egy forgalmas csomópontnál. A falakon túl című műsor első adásában N. Kósa Judit újságíróval, várostörténésszel beszélgetett Morcsányi Elza.

stories.summary: Folyékony festék, kátrány és gépkocsi alkatrészek is voltak szétszórva a nagykátai zöldterületen.

stories.summary: Előzetes megállapodást kötött a szakszervezet és a cégvezetés.

stories.summary: Kivitte a már visszaváltott palackokat a raktárból, aztán újra visszaváltotta azokat.

stories.summary: Csütörtökön vitázott az Európai Parlament a Stop Destroying Videogames nevű kezdeményezésről, még a woke is szóba került.

stories.summary: Szűcs Balázs korábban a Szőlő utcai Javítóintézetben is dolgozott.

stories.summary: Mark Rutte derűlátó a szövetség jövőjével kapcsolatban.

stories.summary: A küszködő német autógyártók egyelőre csak szavak szintjén nyitnak a hadiipar felé, és kérdéses, hogy az átállás mennyire élénkítheti a gazdaságot.

stories.title: Fontos átalakulás jön az egyetemeken, de van, aki szerint 10-15 évvel vetné vissza őket, ha újra a régi rendszer lesz

stories.summary: Lakatos Ádám gyermekvédelmi aktivista súlyos vádakat fogalmazott meg.

stories.title: Airbus-katasztrófa: a gyártót és az Air France légitársaságot is elítélték a 2009-es baleset miatt
stories.summary: 228 ember halt meg a balesetben, a cégek tagadták a bűnösségüket.

stories.title: Május 26-tól hétköznap esténként is autómentessé válik a Pesti alsó rakpart
stories.summary: És ez így lesz szeptember közepéig.

stories.title: Felfüggesztette a Metropol kiadását a Mediaworks
stories.summary: A felfüggesztés okairól és időtartamáról semmilyen további tájékoztatást nem adtak.

stories.title: Az intézményekben gondozottaknak is könnyebbé kell majd tenni a bűncselekmények bejelentését az EU-ban
stories.summary: A gyerekáldozatoknak a legmagasabb szintű védelmet és támogatást kell biztosítani az EP-ben megszavazott, informálisan már véglegesített jogszabályterv alapján.

stories.title: Az ügyvédi kamara szerint több probléma is van a kegyelmi ügy kezelésével
stories.summary: A testület tagjai aggódnak egy kollégájuk pellengérre állítása miatt, de az se tetszik nekik, ahogy ez az ügyvéd kommunikál.

stories.title: Titokzatos támogatók adtak Hont András cégének félmilliárd forintot
stories.summary: Hont szerint az összeg nem egy évre szól, hanem hosszabb távú befektetés és egy fillér állami pénz nincs benne.

stories.title: A bírálatok után a szegedi egyetem felfüggeszti a Közraktárak bontását
stories.summary: Egy hét nyilvánosság után az SZTE egyeztetést kezdeményez Botka László polgármesterrel és Vitézy Dávid miniszterrel.

stories.title: Bejelentette a szakképzésért és felnőttkori tanulásért felelős államtitkárt Lannert Judit
stories.summary: Naderi Zsuzsanna közgazdász, közgazdász-tanár és okleveles könyvvizsgáló. Közel harminc éve foglalkozik szakmai oktatással és képzéssel.

stories.title: Akadozott az államkincstári belépés, sokan lecsúsztak a máig elérhető, még kedvezőbb kamatozású állampapírokról
stories.summary: Olvasónknak azt mondták a TeleKincstárnál, hogy a hiba elhárítása lehet akár néhány óra vagy több nap is.

stories.title: Óda a meztelen férfi testhez, nyíló kertek és lázadó madarak – minden, amit a héten látni, olvasni, hallgatni és érezni érdemes!
stories.summary: Van ideje, de nincs ötlete? Vagy épp alig marad idő kultúrprogramra/kikapcsolódni, és ezért a legjobb döntést szeretné hozni? Segítünk, indul a Karakter heti programajánlója!

stories.title: A választás utáni első pécsi közgyűlésen lépett ki a Fidesz-frakcióból egy képviselő
stories.summary: Háber István Ervin döntését a pécsi Fidesz nemrég megválasztott elnöke jelentette be, aki szerdán még azt mondta: bízik benne, hogy nem lesz lemorzsolódás a frakcióból.

stories.title: Az ukránok szerint megsemmisítettek egy orosz drónpilóta-kiképző tábort
stories.summary: Legalább 65 orosz drónpilóta-kadét és egy kiképző halt meg az ukrán dróncsapásban.

stories.title: Ruszin-Szendi Romulusz: A katonákra költik a Hadtörténeti Múzeum felújítására szánt 18 milliárdot
stories.summary: A minisztérium lehetőséget ad a parancsnokoknak arra, hogy saját objektumaik infrastrukturális fejlesztésére tegyenek javaslatot.

stories.title: Turkey targets press freedom in trial of DW's Alican Uludag
stories.summary: DW correspondent Alican Uludag has been in custody in Turkey for three months, in part on charges of "insulting the president." He is set to appear in court for the first time on Thursday, though only via video link.

stories.title: German authorities arrest 2 on China high-tech espionage allegations
stories.summary: Two Munich residents have been arrested on suspicion of working for a Chinese intelligence service, seeking to obtain and pass on high-tech information with "military applications."

stories.title: Germany records high numbers of online child abuse cases
stories.summary: Efforts to regulate social media have had little effect so far, according to Jugendschutz.net. The government-funded organization says young people in Germany are defenseless against online hate and violence.

stories.title: China’s Xi holds welcome ceremony for Putin
stories.summary: Xi Jinping welcomed Russian President Vladimir Putin with a military ceremony in Beijing.

stories.title: Nigeria arrests former minister in hiding after corruption conviction
stories.summary: The former power minister was sentenced to 75 years in jail in absentia earlier this month.

stories.title: ‘I am not going to walk away’, says Starmer as Burnham pitches debate on ’how politics needs to change’ – as it happened
stories.summary: The prime minister said he wouldn’t be setting out a timetable to stand down if Andy Burnham wins the Makerfield byelection Jakub Krupa writes the Guardian’s Europe live blog. The European Commission has been asked about its response to the renewed talk about Britain’s potential future attempt to rejoin the European Union – but did not take a bait. At this stage, there are discussions on closer cooperation in a number of areas, that’s where we are, and that’s what we are doing precisely in prepa

stories.title: WHO considers use of experimental vaccines as Ebola cases and deaths rise in DRC
stories.summary: WHO chief said he was ‘deeply concerned’ after at least 500 suspected Ebola cases and 130 deaths reported in outbreak of Bundibugyo strain ‘It’s heartbreaking’: panic in eastern DRC over return of Ebola Global health leaders are considering whether vaccines or medicines still in development could be used to fight Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, as the World Health Organization’s chief said he was deeply concerned by the outbreak’s speed and scale. Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus sai

stories.title: Rubio criticizes WHO’s Ebola response as US continues sweeping public health cuts
stories.summary: US secretary of state says WHO was ‘a little late’ in identifying deadly Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, said on Tuesday that the World Health Organization (WHO) was “a little late” in identifying the deadly Ebola outbreak in the the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. On Tuesday, Rubio told reporters: “The lead is obviously going to be CDC [Centers for Disease Control] and the World Health Organization, which was a little late to identify th

stories.title: ‘How are we going to survive this?’ Wellington faces six-month wait to halt sewage spill
stories.summary: Millions of litres of sewage have been spilling into the capital city’s waters since February after the catastrophic failure of a Moa Point wastewater plant A fix to stop millions of litres of sewage continuing to pour into the waters off the coast of New Zealand’s capital, Wellington will be in place by November, officials have said, with full repairs at the cost of NZ$53.5m by late next year. More than 100 days since the catastrophic failure of the city’s wastewater treatment plant on 4 Februa

stories.title: US doctor who contracted Ebola in DRC flown to Germany for treatment
stories.summary: Dr Peter Stafford’s wife and four children are also being monitored for symptoms amid Ebola outbreak in Congo An American doctor who contracted Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has been flown to Germany for treatment, along with his wife and four children, as the World Health Organization warned of the “scale and speed” of the outbreak. Authorities have reported at least 134 suspected deaths and more than 500 cases of the hemorrhagic Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatment

stories.title: Same but different: how Xi and China welcomed Trump and Putin
stories.summary: Choreography of back-to-back visits appeared deliberately mirrored but China made sure the differences were noticed Days after Donald Trump was greeted in Beijing with a military band, an honour guard and dozens of youths waving American and Chinese flags, Vladimir Putin arrived in China to an almost identical spectacle . The choreography of the two welcomes appeared deliberately mirrored, designed to showcase Beijing’s ability to host leaders from Washington and Moscow with equal grandeur. Cont

stories.title: Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing for talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping
stories.summary: Presidents Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin each praised their countries' bilateral relations during the Russian leader's visit to Beijing.

stories.title: EU is failing to cut pesticide use
stories.summary: With binding cut targets scrapped, the European Union's pledge to halve pesticide use is stalling — even as controversial chemicals like glyphosate remain on sale across the bloc.

stories.title: How Meta wants to profile 13-year-olds on Insta, Facebook
stories.summary: Instagram and Facebook have a problem with underage users — there's too many of them. Parent company, Meta, aims to root out under-13s with an age-profiling AI. This plays into a debate about a teenage social media ban.

stories.title: Researchers spot humpback whales off Australia and Brazil — longest lifetime journey on record
stories.summary: Researchers have observed two humpback whales swimming between Australia and Brazil. It's the longest travel distance humans have ever recorded for the species.

stories.title: Why Stephen Colbert's 'Late Show' mattered
stories.summary: As the final curtain falls on "The Late Show," a brief history of the evolution of the late-night tradition and Stephen Colbert's satire.

stories.title: Thailand cuts visa-free stay period for more than 90 countries including UK
stories.summary: Many visitors who have been exempt for 60 days will soon have to apply for a visa after 30 days.

stories.title: Trump endorses Paxton in Texas, gambling on a challenger with baggage in a crucial race
stories.summary: Trump's move dismayed Senate Republicans, many of whom have served with the Texan for decades.
